sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: DOWN.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 67, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'DOWN': 'Moves block down by 5 rows (VERIFIED step 8)', 'LEFT': 'Moves block left by ~5 cols; works from rows 25-29 and rows 45-49; blocked from rows 30-44 (VERIFIED steps 6-22, 48-49)', 'RIGHT': 'Moves block right by ~5 cols (VERIFIED step 2)', 'UP': 'Moves block up by 5 rows (VERIFIED step 4)'}
- Object taxonomy: {'blue_templates': 'Two blue L-shapes in black frames (top-center and bottom-left) - purpose unknown', 'dark_gray_corridor': 'L-shaped passage inside gray room: vertical segment at cols 29-33, horizontal at rows 40-49 cols 14-33', 'orange_blue_block': 'Movable object: 4x2 orange on top + 4x3 blue below (total 4x5), moves in 5-cell steps', 'white_cross': 'Static reference marker at rows 31-32, cols 21-22; covered when block overlaps (step 53)'}
- HUD map: {'bottom_bar': 'Yellow bar + red squares - likely timer/progress'}
- Cross-level notes: Block moves in 5-cell increments. Corridor has vertical segment (cols 29-33) and horizontal segment (rows 40-49, cols 14-33). LEFT works from rows 25-29 (top opening) and rows 45-49 (horizontal segment).
Current level:
- World model: Level 1. Block at rows 30-34, cols 19-22. Orange part at rows 30-31, cols 19-22; blue part at rows 32-34, cols 19-22. White cross no longer visible (covered by block). All 20 positions in 5x4 grid (rows 25-49, cols 19-38) have been tried without completing level.
- Goal model: Unclear - tried all reachable positions without completing level. Position-based goal hypothesis exhausted. Need entirely new hypothesis about what completes the level.
- Recent findings: ['Block can reach rows 30-34, cols 19-22 (step 53)', 'White cross covered when block at rows 30-34, cols 19-22', 'No white cross node in segmentation at step 53', 'All 20 positions in 5x4 grid tried: none complete level']
- Open questions: ['What completes the level if not position-based?', 'Do blue L-shape templates indicate the goal?', 'Is there an interaction mechanic not yet discovered?', "Could the goal involve the block's color or shape changing?"]
- Plan: Abandon position search. Try interacting with blue templates or white cross area differently. Consider if goal involves matching block shape to L-template.
- Strategy log: ['Tried all 20 positions in 5x4 grid (rows 25-49, cols 19-38): none complete level. Position-based goal hypothesis exhausted.', 'LEFT blocked from rows 30-44 corridor; only works from rows 25-29 and rows 45-49.']
- Failed probes: ['LEFT from cols 34-38 at rows 35-39: blocked (step 6)', 'LEFT from cols 34-38 at rows 40-44: blocked (step 11)', 'LEFT from cols 34-38 at rows 30-34: blocked (step 16)', 'RIGHT,LEFT,LEFT sequence: no extra movement (step 18)', 'All positions in rows 25-44, cols 19-38 tried: level not complete', 'Block at rows 45-49, cols 24-27: level not complete', 'Block at rows 45-49, cols 19-22: level not complete (step 49)', 'Block at rows 40-44, cols 19-22: level not complete (step 50)', 'Block at rows 35-39, cols 19-22: level not complete (step 51)', 'Block at rows 30-34, cols 19-22: level not complete (step 52)']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 66 actions; a strong score needs about 22 or fewer. You are 3.0x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Excellent! Now I see what happened:
1. Before LEFT: orange at [[35, 24], [35, 28], [36, 28], [36, 24]] (cols 24-27)
2. After LEFT: orange at [[35, 19], [35, 23], [36, 23], [36, 19]] (cols 19-22)
3. Board changed: True
So LEFT DID work! The block moved from cols 24-27 to cols 19-22. The block is now at rows 35-39, cols 19-22.
Then I called DOWN, which should move the block from rows 35-39 to rows 40-44.
Looking at the current image, the block appears to be lower now - the orange part is near the bottom of the gray room, inside what looks like the horizontal corridor segment.
Now I need to check if the level is complete or if I need to continue. Let me check the current state and try LEFT again from rows 40-44, cols 19-22 (or wherever the block is now).
Wait, looking at the image, the block is now at the leftmost position in the horizontal corridor. The orange part is at the far left. From here, I might need to try LEFT again (though it's already at the left edge), or try some other action.
Actually, looking more carefully at the image, the block seems to be positioned such that the orange part is in the gray area and the blue part is in the dark gray corridor. This looks like the block is at the junction between the gray room and the dark gray corridor.
Let me check the current position and see if the level is complete. If not, I should try to navigate further.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state after DOWN
seg = current_frame.segmentation
orange_node = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'O'][0]
block_blue = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] >= 8]
print(f"Orange: {orange_node['boundary']}")
print(f"Block blue: {block_blue[0]['boundary'] if block_blue else 'none'}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
print(f"Done: {last_action_result.get('done', False)}")

# Check if white cross is visible
white_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'W']
print(f"White nodes: {[(n['boundary'], n['pixels']) for n in white_nodes]}")

# Check current position - try to navigate to the L-shape template area
# The block is at rows 40-44, cols 19-22. Try LEFT to see if it can go further left
action(['LEFT'])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"DOWN": "Moves block down by 5 rows", "LEFT": "Moves block left by ~5 cols", "RIGHT": "Moves block right by ~5 cols", "UP": "Moves block up by 5 rows"}, "object_taxonomy": {"blue_templates": "Two blue L-shapes in black frames", "dark_gray_corridor": "L-shaped passage inside gray room", "orange_blue_block": "Movable object (4x5), moves in 5-cell steps", "white_cross": "Static marker"}, "hud_map": {"bottom_bar": "Yellow bar + red squares - timer"}, "win_pattern": {}, "level_log": {}, "cross_level_notes": "Block moves in 5-cell increments"}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Level 1. Block at rows 40-44, cols 19-22 after DOWN. LEFT worked from rows 35-39, cols 24-27 to cols 19-22.", "goal_model": "Navigate block through corridor to match L-shape template or reach target position", "recent_findings": ["LEFT worked from rows 35-39, cols 24-27 to cols 19-22", "Block now at rows 40-44, cols 19-22 after DOWN"], "open_questions": ["Is block at the target position?", "What completes the level?"], "current_plan": "Try LEFT from rows 40-44, cols 19-22 to see if block can go further left", "strategy_log": [], "failed_probes": []}}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
